category_title: Students using systematic decomposition and goal-oriented strategies
scenario_title: Navigating high textual complexity in a four-step word problem
minimal_context: Benjamin and Konstantin are using the Word Problem Cracker tool to write a complex expression for a four-step word problem about costs for meals, drinks, and bowling.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's work on this four-step word problem about the costs for meals, drinks, and bowling. Please try to mathematize it by writing a complex expression.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Six euros times persons, ok. What do I get then? …. the costs of the pizza.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Okay, what do you think you should do next?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I am not going to write in the trial and error space. Let's just switch between the columns and start with the easiest aspect.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: How will you put the rest of the costs together into the expression?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: This would be 6 times … well, this here and this here (hints to costs for meals and drinks and bowling). There we must first … mmhm … now we have to fill yellow and blue before writing the expression.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's work on this four-step word problem about the costs for meals, drinks, and bowling. Please try to mathematize it by writing a complex expression.

STUDENT: Six euros times persons, ok. What do I get then? …. the costs of the pizza.

TUTOR: Okay, what do you think you should do next?

STUDENT: I am not going to write in the trial and error space. Let's just switch between the columns and start with the easiest aspect.

TUTOR: How will you put the rest of the costs together into the expression?

STUDENT: This would be 6 times … well, this here and this here (hints to costs for meals and drinks and bowling). There we must first … mmhm … now we have to fill yellow and blue before writing the expression.

task.content: Work on a four-step word problem with high textual complexity involving costs for meals and drinks and bowling, and mathematize by a complex expression
task.learning_goal: Focus on the meaning of the variable and the mental construction of quantities as well as the final questions as part of working forward and backward

context.environment: Working on mathematization and comprehension processes
context.materials: Word Problem Cracker (WPC) tool, featuring yellow and blue columns (left and middle column) and a trial and error space
context.social_setting: Two boys (Benjamin and Konstantin) working together

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When attempting to combine multiple pieces of information (costs for meals, drinks, and bowling) into a single subexpression
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Hesitates, says 'mmhm', hints to different parts of the problem text ('this here and this here'), and delays writing the expression to focus on filling columns first
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Experiencing high textual complexity requiring strong control by strategy S3
related_quotes: This would be 6 times … well, this here and this here [hints to costs for meals and drinks and bowling]. There we must first … mmhm … now we have to fill yellow and blue [i.e. the left and middle column] before writing the expression.

behavior_patterns.condition: When navigating the columns of the scaffolding tool
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Switch between the order of columns quite flexibly, always starting with the easiest aspect

behavior_patterns.condition: When working forward through the problem pathway
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Often ask, 'What do we get? What does it mean?' with a consequent focus on quantities
related_quotes: Six euros times persons, ok. What do I get then? …. the costs of the pizza

behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Untypically, they do not use the trial and error space, whereas other students tentatively write sub-expressions in it; they do not repeat the steps made in the trial and error space if they are successful, but continue their way forward
